title: Improvement in telegraphy
abstract: An apparatus for transmitting signals over wires using an electromagnet to mark intelligible characters at a distance.

title: Improvement in sewing-machines
abstract: A machine for stitching cloth using a continuously moving needle paired with a shuttle bobbin.

title: Improvement in telegraphy (lock-and-key)
abstract: A method of multiplexing telegraphic messages so that several can share a single wire.

title: Improvement in sewing-machines (Singer)
abstract: A foot-pedal-driven sewing machine with a vertical needle and a horizontal feed mechanism for domestic use.

title: Process of vulcanizing rubber
abstract: A process of treating rubber with sulphur under heat to produce a stable elastic material suitable for industrial use.

title: Improvement in dynamo-electric machines
abstract: A direct-current generator using a ring-wound armature for producing electrical power from rotational motion.

title: Improvement in telephony (Bell)
abstract: An apparatus for transmitting vocal sounds telegraphically by varying the resistance of a circuit in proportion to acoustic vibrations.

title: Improvement in carbon-button telephone transmitters
abstract: A microphone for telephone use that varies electrical resistance in proportion to acoustic pressure on a carbon button.

title: Phonograph or speaking machine
abstract: A device that records and reproduces sound by inscribing acoustic vibrations onto a rotating cylinder of tinfoil.

title: Electric lamp
abstract: An incandescent electric lamp using a high-resistance carbonized filament in an evacuated glass bulb.

title: Improvement in incandescent lamps
abstract: An improved long-lived filament lamp using a treated bamboo filament for general illumination.

title: Improvement in electric distribution systems
abstract: A three-wire direct-current distribution system that reduces conductor weight in commercial-scale electric power networks.

title: Electric transmission of power
abstract: An alternating-current induction motor driven by a polyphase rotating magnetic field.

title: System of electrical distribution
abstract: A polyphase alternating-current system for the long-distance transmission of electrical power.

title: Improvement in elevators
abstract: A safety brake mechanism that automatically arrests an elevator car if the hoist cable fails.

title: Improvement in pneumatic tires
abstract: An inflatable rubber tire mounted on a metal rim for use on bicycles and carriages.

title: Internal-combustion engine (Diesel)
abstract: A high-compression engine in which fuel is ignited by the heat of the compressed air alone, without a spark.

title: Method of and apparatus for controlling mechanism of moving vessels
abstract: An automatic remote control system for guided vehicles using radio signals.

title: Apparatus for transmitting electrical signals
abstract: A wireless telegraphy apparatus using a coherer and an antenna for receiving Hertzian waves at a distance.

title: Flying machine
abstract: A heavier-than-air flying machine controlled by warping the wings to maintain lateral balance in flight.

title: Device for amplifying feeble electrical currents
abstract: A three-electrode vacuum tube that amplifies electrical signals by controlling current flow with a grid.

title: Process of manufacturing aluminum
abstract: An electrolytic process for reducing aluminum oxide dissolved in molten cryolite.

title: Process for making phenol-formaldehyde resins (Bakelite)
abstract: A method of making a hard thermosetting plastic by condensing phenol with formaldehyde under heat and pressure.

title: Aspirin
abstract: A process for the synthesis of acetylsalicylic acid as a stable orally administered analgesic.

title: Cathode-ray tube
abstract: A vacuum tube in which a beam of electrons is deflected by magnetic or electric fields to form an image on a phosphor screen.

title: Television system
abstract: A method of electronically scanning an image into a sequence of electrical signals using an image-dissector tube.

title: Direct-view storage tube (Iconoscope)
abstract: A camera tube that converts an optical image into an electrical signal using a mosaic of photoelectric cells.

title: Synthetic linear condensation polymers (Nylon)
abstract: A process for producing strong synthetic fibers from polyamides by melt-spinning under tension.

title: Direct-lift aircraft (helicopter)
abstract: A single-rotor helicopter using a tail rotor for torque compensation and cyclic pitch for control.

title: Frequency modulation system
abstract: A method of transmitting audio signals by varying the frequency of a carrier wave to reduce noise.

title: High-frequency oscillator
abstract: A regenerative oscillator that uses positive feedback to produce stable radio-frequency oscillations.

title: Refrigeration apparatus
abstract: A vapor-compression refrigeration cycle using a non-flammable, non-toxic chlorofluorocarbon refrigerant.

title: Air-conditioning system
abstract: An apparatus for treating air to control its temperature, humidity, and circulation in indoor spaces.

title: Light-amplification by stimulated emission of radiation (laser)
abstract: A device that produces a coherent beam of light by stimulated emission in a resonant optical cavity.

title: Maser apparatus
abstract: A device that amplifies microwave radiation by stimulated emission from an inverted-population gas medium.

title: Junction transistor
abstract: A solid-state amplifier using a thin layer of semiconductor sandwiched between two regions of opposite type.

title: Three-electrode circuit element using semiconductive materials
abstract: A point-contact transistor amplifier consisting of two metal contacts on a germanium crystal.

title: Integrated circuit (Kilby)
abstract: A miniature electronic circuit in which all components are formed in and on a single piece of semiconductor material.

title: Planar integrated circuit (Noyce)
abstract: A monolithic integrated circuit fabricated using a planar oxide-masking process for interconnections.

title: Memory system using magnetic cores
abstract: A random-access memory using arrays of toroidal magnetic cores threaded by drive and sense wires.

title: Drum memory device
abstract: A magnetic data-storage device using a rotating cylinder coated with ferromagnetic material.

title: Random-access dynamic memory (DRAM)
abstract: A single-transistor memory cell that stores a bit as charge on a capacitor and refreshes periodically.

title: Memory system having direct random access (Forrester)
abstract: A coincident-current core memory permitting parallel readout of multi-bit words.

title: Microprogrammed CPU on a chip (Intel 4004)
abstract: A 4-bit microprocessor integrating arithmetic, control, and memory interface on a single MOS chip.

title: Single-chip microcomputer
abstract: A single integrated circuit combining processor, memory, and input/output for embedded control applications.

title: Method of optical character recognition
abstract: A scanning method for converting printed alphanumeric characters into digital codes by template matching.

title: Bar-code identification system
abstract: A pattern of parallel bars of varying widths that encode product identifiers for optical scanning at point of sale.

title: Polaroid one-step photographic process
abstract: A self-developing instant photographic film that produces a finished print within one minute of exposure.

title: Xerographic copier
abstract: An electrostatic dry-imaging process for duplicating documents onto plain paper using a photoconductive drum.

title: Microwave cooking apparatus
abstract: An oven that heats food by exposing it to high-frequency electromagnetic radiation generated by a magnetron.

title: Hook-and-loop fastener (Velcro)
abstract: A fastening tape consisting of two strips, one with stiff hooks and the other with soft loops that mesh on contact.

title: Disposable diaper
abstract: A multi-layer absorbent disposable diaper with a moisture-impermeable backing and adhesive tabs.

title: Integrated stereo headphone
abstract: A high-fidelity dynamic stereo headphone with foam ear cushions for personal listening.

title: Radial keratotomy
abstract: A surgical method of correcting myopia by making radial incisions in the cornea.

title: Implantable cardiac pacemaker
abstract: A self-contained, battery-powered electronic device implanted to regulate the heartbeat by delivering pulses to the myocardium.

title: Computed tomography apparatus
abstract: A medical imaging system that reconstructs cross-sectional images of the body from a rotating array of X-ray projections.

title: Apparatus and method for nuclear-magnetic-resonance imaging
abstract: A medical imaging system that uses nuclear-magnetic-resonance signals to produce sectional images of soft tissue.

title: Process for amplifying nucleic acid sequences (PCR)
abstract: A method of exponentially amplifying specific DNA segments using thermostable polymerase and primer pairs.

title: Recombinant DNA cloning vehicle
abstract: A method of producing recombinant DNA molecules by inserting foreign genes into a plasmid vector for bacterial expression.

title: Genetically modified bacterium
abstract: A bacterium genetically engineered to digest crude-oil hydrocarbons, the first patentable life form upheld in Diamond v. Chakrabarty.

title: Inkjet printing apparatus
abstract: A printer that forms images by ejecting droplets of ink from a thermally driven nozzle array onto paper.

title: Liquid-crystal display device
abstract: A flat-panel display that modulates light by controlling the orientation of liquid-crystal molecules between polarizers.

title: Light-emitting diode device
abstract: A semiconductor diode that emits visible light when current is passed across its junction.

title: Compact disc digital audio system
abstract: A digital audio storage medium that encodes sound as pits on a reflective optical disc read by a laser.

title: Magnetic resonance imaging gradient coil
abstract: A gradient-coil assembly producing spatially varying magnetic fields for slice selection in MRI scanners.

title: Mouse for use with a computer system
abstract: A hand-held pointing device that controls a screen cursor by detecting motion in two orthogonal axes.

title: Method and apparatus for graphical user interfaces
abstract: A windowed graphical user interface for personal computers using overlapping windows, icons, and menus.

title: Electronic spreadsheet program
abstract: A computer program that arranges numerical data in a grid of formula-linked cells for interactive recalculation.

title: Public-key cryptographic apparatus and method (RSA)
abstract: A cryptographic system that uses a public encryption key and a private decryption key derived from large prime numbers.

title: Method for transmitting data using packet switching
abstract: A method of transmitting digital messages by breaking them into independently routed packets across a shared network.

title: Hypertext document linking system (Web)
abstract: A method of linking documents across a distributed network using uniform resource identifiers and a hypertext protocol.

title: Method for arranging frames in a continuously rolling display
abstract: A scrolling method for displaying ranked search results within a paged web interface.

title: PageRank ranking method
abstract: A method of ranking documents in a hyperlinked corpus based on the eigenvector of the link-citation matrix.

title: Direct-broadcast satellite television receiver
abstract: A consumer receiver for decoding digital television signals broadcast directly from geostationary satellites.

title: Cellular mobile telephone system
abstract: A radiotelephone system that hands off calls between geographic cells as the mobile unit moves.

title: Code division multiple access (CDMA) system
abstract: A spread-spectrum digital cellular system that distinguishes simultaneous users by orthogonal code sequences.

title: Wireless local area network (Wi-Fi)
abstract: A method of providing high-rate wireless data networking using orthogonal frequency-division multiplexing in unlicensed bands.

title: Bluetooth short-range radio system
abstract: A short-range, low-power radio protocol for ad-hoc data and audio links between consumer devices.

title: Lithium-ion rechargeable battery
abstract: A rechargeable battery cell using a lithium-intercalation cathode and a carbonaceous anode in a non-aqueous electrolyte.

title: Nickel-metal-hydride storage battery
abstract: A rechargeable battery cell using a hydrogen-absorbing alloy as the negative electrode for high energy density.

title: Photovoltaic solar cell
abstract: A silicon photovoltaic cell that converts sunlight directly into electrical current at usable efficiencies.

title: Wind turbine with variable-speed control
abstract: A horizontal-axis wind turbine with electronic variable-speed control for maximum power extraction.

title: Catalytic converter
abstract: An automotive exhaust aftertreatment device that converts pollutants into harmless gases over a noble-metal catalyst.

title: Anti-lock braking system
abstract: An automotive braking system that prevents wheel lockup by modulating brake pressure under threshold detection.

title: Airbag restraint system
abstract: A vehicle safety system that rapidly inflates a fabric cushion in response to a collision-detection accelerometer.

title: Three-point safety belt
abstract: A vehicle restraint that crosses both the chest and lap of an occupant from a single retractor anchor.

title: Hybrid electric vehicle drivetrain
abstract: A drivetrain combining an internal-combustion engine with an electric motor and battery pack for improved fuel economy.

title: Jet propulsion engine
abstract: A turbojet engine using a gas-turbine compressor to provide reaction thrust for high-speed aircraft propulsion.

title: Liquid-fueled rocket engine
abstract: A rocket motor that burns a liquid oxidizer and a liquid fuel in a regeneratively-cooled combustion chamber.

title: Communication satellite system
abstract: A geostationary communications satellite that relays radio signals between widely separated ground stations.

title: Global positioning system receiver
abstract: A receiver that determines its position by measuring the time-of-arrival of signals from a constellation of satellites.

title: Industrial robot manipulator
abstract: A computer-controlled multi-axis robotic arm capable of performing repetitive industrial tasks with high precision.

title: Numerical control system for machine tools
abstract: A system for controlling a machine tool by reading numerical instructions from a punched tape.

title: Three-dimensional printing apparatus
abstract: A method of producing solid objects by selectively depositing or solidifying successive layers of material.

title: Stereolithography apparatus
abstract: A method of forming three-dimensional objects by selectively photopolymerizing successive layers of liquid resin.

title: Fiber-optic transmission cable
abstract: A communication cable using ultra-pure glass fibers as low-loss waveguides for modulated light signals.

title: Erbium-doped fiber amplifier
abstract: An optical amplifier that boosts signals in fiber-optic networks using a rare-earth-doped fiber pumped by a diode laser.

title: Photolithographic stepper
abstract: A semiconductor manufacturing apparatus that exposes patterns onto a wafer one die at a time using projection optics.

title: Electroencephalograph (EEG) machine
abstract: A medical instrument that records the electrical activity of the brain by amplifying scalp-electrode signals.

title: Process for producing penicillin in deep tank fermentation
abstract: A submerged-fermentation process for the large-scale production of penicillin from Penicillium chrysogenum cultures.
